column3: This file should be populated with ALL Provider payment records that edifecs received  from HPSCIL/HRP, and populate the status of each record to denote if they are  succesfully converted to 835 and transmitted to Payment vendor. PULSE360 application needs this information to identify the fallout of data that happened within edifecs and thus to balance the inbound and outbound data within the ecosystem.

UST_<EDI_Converter>_<PaymentVendor_Name>_ProviderPayment_<Healthplan>_<Frequency>_<generatedTimeStamp>.csv
Example: UST_Edifecs_PNC_ProviderPayment_HHO_<Daily>_12082021144200.csv
csv
Description

Name of the Electronic health data exchange tool which recieves Payment records from HRP/CIL sources and convert them to 835 format
Example - Edifecs/Mercator etc
column8: This is a must have data and if this field is not provided by the EDI convertor, PULSE dashboard will not provide a categorical view with respect to vendor name

column3: Member payment / provider payment
column8: This field is required to differentiate if the payment feed is for Member payment or provider payment.

column3: The name of file(Outbound)  in which the EDI convertor sent Payment Data in 835 EDI to the payment vendor
column8:
This is a Must have field for PULSE to calculate the transaction requests per file per day.
This filename should help to segregate the list of distinct files received from source and hence it is recommended to add created/received timestamp in the name for uniqueness of file name.

column3: Name of the file(Inbound to edi convertor) in which payment information is received from HRP
column8: File name is required to group records according to each file and do the balancing.

column3: Frequency of the file
column8: Must be populated with the source file frequency.

column3: Key Identifier column to track the payment transaction status
column8: Key Identifier  column which helps to track the transaction end to end. This field is displayed in the front end.

column3: Date of the Check Issued

column3: Payment Amount in the check
column8: This is a key field that needs to be displayed in the front end

column3: Unique ID for each claim generated within HRP.
column8: Needs to be shown in the front end

column3: Unique ID of a patient in a claim
column8: Needs to be shown in the front end

column3: Name of Patient
column8: Needs to be shown in the front end

column3: Name of the bank which processes the payment
column8: This field needs to be displayed in the front end

column3: Accepted/Rejected/On-Hold/Other status (if any)
column7: A or R or H or O
column8:
A - Accepted -For good records that can be pushed to the Claims Adjudication process
R - Rejected - For bad records
H - OnHold - For records under review by the Vendor
O - Other

This field help to categorise the records into different Data check points for the balancing purpose. Major field which is a must have for the PULSE Dashboard to perform Balancing

column3: Rejection Code , if the record is not successfully converted to 835

column3: Reason for rejection
column8: To be populated with the first encountered error for rejected records only . This error description will be displayed in PULSE dashboard and is a must have field for rejected records.
